issues.issue: Option 'pergi' is not in lesson 1 vocabulary (learning-items.ts). The correct answer uses a word the learner has not been formally introduced to.
issues.fix: Replace 'pergi' with a lesson 1 verb (e.g. 'makan') and adjust sentence/translation accordingly, or add 'pergi' to learning-items.ts

issues.issue: Distractors include Dutch words ('kunnen', 'te', 'om') mixed into Indonesian phrases. While pedagogically intentional (testing L1 interference), this differs from the vocabulary-based distractor approach used elsewhere.
issues.fix: Admin review: confirm this is the desired approach for serial-verb cloze_mcq items. These test construction rather than vocabulary, so phrase-level distractors may be appropriate.

issues.issue: All three zero-copula cloze_mcq candidates use the identical distractor set (mahal, murah, besar, baik) in different orders. Learner may memorise the option set rather than engage with each sentence.
issues.fix: Admin may want to vary distractors across the three items (e.g. substitute 'cukup' or 'kosong' for one item)

issues.issue: Identical option sets: nanas, buah, pisang, rumah. When the learner encounters both exercises, they may feel repetitive.
issues.fix: Admin may want to swap one distractor in one of the two items for variety (e.g. 'harga' or 'hotel')

issues.issue: Distractors 'bukan' and 'jangan' are not in lesson 1 vocabulary. However, they are functionally appropriate negation particles that form a natural contrast set with 'tidak' and 'belum'.
issues.fix: No action needed if admin is comfortable introducing these as distractors before formal teaching. They serve as functional-category distractors which is pedagogically sound.

issues.issue: confusion_group is null despite conceptual overlap with serial-verb-construction (both deal with verb form invariance)
issues.fix: Consider adding a shared confusion_group if learners confuse these patterns in practice
